SEARCH
Module project
Package project.pomodoro

Class ButtonController

java.lang.Object
project.pomodoro.ButtonController
public class ButtonController extends Object
Controller class for handling button interactions in a Pomodoro timer application. Manages user actions such as starting, pausing, and resetting the timer, as well as switching between the Pomodoro and break views.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ButtonController()

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected void
    breakBtnClick()
    Handles the click event for the break button.
    void
    pauseBtnClick()
    Handles the click event for the pause button.
    protected void

    Handles the click event for the Pomodoro button.
    void

    Handles the click event for the reset button.

    Handles the click event for the start button.

  • Constructor Details

    • ButtonController

      public ButtonController()

  • Method Details

    • startBtnClick

      public void startBtnClick()
      Handles the click event for the start button. Resumes the timer if it is paused.

    • pauseBtnClick

      public void pauseBtnClick()
      Handles the click event for the pause button. Pauses the currently running timer.

    • resetBtnClick

      public void resetBtnClick()
      Handles the click event for the reset button. Resets the timer to its initial state.

    • pomoBtnClick

      protected void pomoBtnClick() throws Exception
      Handles the click event for the Pomodoro button. Switches the scene to the Pomodoro timer view with a 25-minute duration.
      Throws:
      Exception - if there is an error loading the FXML or switching scenes

    • breakBtnClick

      protected void breakBtnClick() throws Exception
      Handles the click event for the break button. Switches the scene to the break timer view with a 5-minute duration.
      Throws:
      Exception - if there is an error loading the FXML or switching scenes
